description: Performance Benchmark Suite — Compare TypoKit across all platform×server combinations against competitor frameworks with bombardier load generation, CI regression detection, and docs site integration

userStories.title: Benchmark package scaffolding and shared fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the packages/benchmarks/ package with shared fixtures, types, and database seed data so that all benchmark apps have a consistent foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/ as a private package with package.json (private: true) and project.json using nx:run-commands executor

Create packages/benchmarks/src/types.ts with shared TypeScript types: BenchmarkResult (framework, platform, server, scenario, reqPerSec, latency percentiles, errors, timestamp, systemInfo), SystemInfo (os, cpu, ram, runtimeVersions), BenchmarkConfig (connections, duration, warmup, runs)

Create packages/benchmarks/src/shared/ with: response shape (static JSON object with 8+ fields including nested object, array, enum, optional), validation schema (moderately complex: nested object, string, number, enum, array, optional fields), and database table definition
Create packages/benchmarks/fixtures/ with: schema.sql (SQLite CREATE TABLE), seed.sql (1,000 rows of test data), and a pre-seeded benchmark.sqlite file
Create packages/benchmarks/src/shared/validation-schema.ts exporting the validation shape as a TypeScript interface with JSDoc annotations matching TypoKit's schema-first pattern
Add packages/benchmarks to pnpm-workspace.yaml if not auto-detected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: bombardier download and cache module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bombardier to be automatically downloaded and cached so that the benchmark suite works out of the box without manual installation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/bombardier.ts that auto-downloads the bombardier binary from GitHub releases on first run
Detect platform (linux/darwin/windows) and architecture (amd64/arm64) to download the correct binary
Cache the binary in node_modules/.cache/benchmarks/bombardier (create directory if needed)
Skip download if cached binary already exists and is executable
Export a getBombardierPath() function that returns the path to the cached binary (downloading first if needed)

Export a runBombardier(url, config) function that spawns bombardier with JSON output, parses results, and returns BenchmarkResult with: req/s avg, latency p50/p95/p99/p99.9, transfer/sec, total requests, errors
Default config: 100 connections, 30s duration; CI config: 50 connections, 10s duration
If download fails, exit with clear error message and manual installation instructions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TypoKit Node.js benchmark apps (4 server adapters)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypoKit benchmark apps for all 4 server adapters on Node.js so that we can measure the Node platform matrix.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-node-native.ts implementing all 5 endpoints: GET /json, POST /validate, GET /db/:id, GET /middleware (5 no-op middleware layers), GET /startup
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-node-fastify.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-fastify
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-node-hono.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-hono
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-node-express.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-express
All apps use @typokit/platform-node, @typokit/core for app creation, and the shared validation schema and DB fixtures
All apps use better-sqlite3 for the /db/:id endpoint with the shared benchmark.sqlite
Each app exports a start() function returning { port: number, close: () => void } with dynamic port allocation (port 0)
GET /json returns the shared static JSON response shape
POST /validate validates the request body against the shared schema and returns the validated object
GET /middleware passes through 5 no-op middleware layers before returning JSON
GET /startup returns { uptime: process.uptime() }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TypoKit Bun benchmark apps (4 server adapters)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypoKit benchmark apps for all 4 server adapters on Bun so that we can measure the Bun platform matrix.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-bun-native.ts implementing all 5 endpoints using @typokit/platform-bun and @typokit/server-native
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-bun-fastify.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-fastify on Bun
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-bun-hono.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-hono on Bun
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-bun-express.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-express via Bun compatibility layer
All apps use Bun's built-in bun:sqlite for the /db/:id endpoint with the shared benchmark.sqlite
Each app exports a start() function returning { port: number, close: () => void } with dynamic port allocation
All 5 endpoints (json, validate, db, middleware, startup) match the same behavior as the Node.js apps
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TypoKit Deno benchmark apps (4 server adapters)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypoKit benchmark apps for all 4 server adapters on Deno so that we can measure the Deno platform matrix.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-deno-native.ts implementing all 5 endpoints using @typokit/platform-deno and @typokit/server-native
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-deno-fastify.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-fastify on Deno
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-deno-hono.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-hono on Deno
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-deno-express.ts with same 5 endpoints using @typokit/server-express via Deno npm: compatibility
All apps use @db/sqlite (or Deno's SQLite module) for the /db/:id endpoint with the shared benchmark.sqlite
Each app exports a start() function returning { port: number, close: () => void } with dynamic port allocation
All 5 endpoints (json, validate, db, middleware, startup) match the same behavior as the Node.js apps
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TypoKit Rust/Axum benchmark app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TypoKit Rust/Axum benchmark app generated via @typokit/plugin-axum so that we can measure the Rust codegen target performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/typokit-rust-axum/ directory with a Cargo.toml and Rust source files
Generate the Rust server code from the same TypeScript validation schema used by JS apps via @typokit/plugin-axum codegen
Implement all 5 endpoints: GET /json, POST /validate, GET /db/:id (using rusqlite), GET /middleware (5 no-op Axum middleware layers), GET /startup
The app uses the same shared benchmark.sqlite fixture (copied at runtime) and returns identical JSON response shapes
The app compiles with cargo build --release successfully
The compiled binary accepts a --port flag or PORT env var for dynamic port allocation
Add a build step in project.json that runs cargo build --release for the Rust app
Typecheck passes for any TypeScript wrapper/runner code

userStories.title: Raw baseline benchmark apps (Node, Bun, Deno, Axum)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need raw baseline benchmark apps using bare runtime APIs so that we can isolate framework overhead from runtime performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/raw-node.ts using raw Node.js http.createServer with all 5 endpoints (no framework, hand-written routing and JSON parsing)
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/raw-bun.ts using raw Bun.serve() with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/raw-deno.ts using raw Deno.serve() with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/raw-axum/ using hand-written Axum (no TypoKit codegen) with all 5 endpoints and rusqlite
All apps use the same shared JSON response shape, validation logic (hand-written if/typeof checks), and SQLite fixture
Each app exports start() or compiles to a binary with the same interface as the TypoKit apps
Raw Axum app compiles with cargo build --release successfully
Typecheck passes for TypeScript apps

userStories.title: Competitor framework benchmark apps group 1 (Express, Fastify, Hono, Koa, Elysia)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need benchmark apps for popular JS frameworks so that we can compare TypoKit against the competition.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-express.ts using standalone Express with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-fastify.ts using standalone Fastify with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-hono.ts using standalone Hono with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-koa.ts using standalone Koa with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-elysia.ts using standalone Elysia (Bun) with all 5 endpoints
All apps use the same shared JSON response shape, equivalent validation logic using each framework's recommended approach, and better-sqlite3 (or bun:sqlite for Elysia)
Each app exports a start() function returning { port, close } with dynamic port allocation
Add framework dependencies to packages/benchmarks/package.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Competitor framework benchmark apps group 2 (tRPC, NestJS, Nitro/H3, Adonis)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need benchmark apps for additional JS frameworks so that the comparison covers the full field.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-trpc.ts using tRPC with all 5 endpoints (using standalone adapter)
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-nestjs.ts using NestJS with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-h3.ts using Nitro/H3 with all 5 endpoints
Create packages/benchmarks/src/apps/competitor-adonis.ts using AdonisJS with all 5 endpoints
All apps use the same shared JSON response shape, equivalent validation using each framework's recommended approach, and better-sqlite3 for database
Each app exports a start() function returning { port, close } with dynamic port allocation
Add framework dependencies to packages/benchmarks/package.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Benchmark runner core and Nx CLI targets
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a benchmark runner that orchestrates server lifecycle and load generation, exposed as Nx targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/runner.ts that orchestrates: start server → poll /json until healthy → run warmup → run bombardier → collect results → stop server → next app
The runner accepts config: scenario filter (json/validate/db/middleware/startup), framework filter (glob pattern), connections, duration, warmup, number of runs to average
The runner outputs progress to stdout with framework name, scenario, and a clean summary table at the end
Results are written to packages/benchmarks/results/ as timestamped JSON files (ISO 8601 in filename)
Define Nx targets in packages/benchmarks/project.json: bench, bench-ci, bench-baseline, bench-info, bench-reproduce — all using nx:run-commands executor with cwd: '{projectRoot}'
The bench target declares dependsOn: ['^build'] so all TypoKit packages are built first
bench-ci uses CI config (50 connections, 10s duration, 5s warmup, 1 run) with a fast subset of frameworks
Add root package.json scripts: pnpm bench → nx run benchmarks:bench, pnpm bench:ci → nx run benchmarks:bench-ci, pnpm bench:baseline, pnpm bench:info, pnpm bench:reproduce
Handle cross-runtime process management: Node apps started directly, Bun via 'bun run', Deno via 'deno run', Rust via compiled binary subprocess
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Results aggregation and latest.json cumulative merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want benchmark results aggregated into structured JSON with cumulative merging so that partial runs preserve previous data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/results.ts that aggregates raw bombardier output into BenchmarkResult objects
Generate a summary markdown table after each run grouped by scenario with columns: Rank, Framework, Platform, Server, Req/s, Latency p50/p95/p99, vs. Fastest (%)
Implement cumulative latest.json merge: new results overwrite matching framework+platform+server+scenario entries; unmatched entries from existing latest.json are preserved
Support averaging results from multiple runs (configurable --runs N) to reduce noise
Results JSON includes systemInfo: OS, CPU model+cores, RAM, runtime versions (Node/Bun/Deno with pinned versions), bombardier version, timestamp
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: System info collection and reproducibility commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want bench-info and bench-reproduce commands for transparency and reproducibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/system-info.ts that detects: OS name+version, CPU model+cores, total RAM, Node/Bun/Deno versions (if available), Rust/cargo version (if available), bombardier version
bench-info Nx target prints system info in JSON format to stdout
bench-reproduce Nx target prints step-by-step reproduction instructions: prerequisites, install commands, exact pnpm bench invocation with parameters matching published results
System info is automatically included in every results JSON file
Deno version is pinned to latest stable at suite creation time and documented in output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validation overhead isolation variants
userStories.description: As an engineer, I need validation variants for TypoKit apps so that we can measure the exact overhead of type-driven validation.
acceptanceCriteria: For each TypoKit benchmark app (all 13 combinations), add two additional /validate endpoint variants: (a) validation disabled/passthrough mode that skips validation and returns the body directly, (b) hand-written if/typeof validation baseline
The runner supports running these variants as sub-scenarios: --scenario validate-passthrough, --scenario validate-handwritten
Results clearly show the delta: 'TypoKit validation adds X% overhead vs. passthrough' and 'TypoKit validation is X% faster/slower than hand-written'
Deltas are included in the results JSON and markdown summary table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Serialization microbenchmark
userStories.description: As an engineer, I need a non-HTTP serialization benchmark to measure TypoKit's compiled serializer vs JSON.stringify in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/benchmarks/src/microbench/serialization.ts that benchmarks JSON serialization of a complex nested object (the shared response shape)
Compare: TypoKit's compiled serializer vs. native JSON.stringify vs. fast-json-stringify
Run N iterations (configurable, default 1,000,000) and report: ops/sec, mean time, p99 time
Results are included in the main results JSON under a 'microbenchmarks' key
Accessible via nx run benchmarks:bench --scenario serialization
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CI workflow with PR regression and main branch full suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a GitHub Actions workflow that catches regressions on PRs and keeps docs benchmarks current on main.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .github/workflows/benchmark.yml with two trigger modes: pull_request and push to main
Both modes use nx affected to scope benchmarks to only those whose upstream dependencies changed; skip entirely if nothing is affected
PR mode runs bench-ci (fast subset) and compares results against benchmarks/baseline.json; fails if any TypoKit combination degrades by more than 10% in req/s
PR mode posts a comment on the PR with a summary table of results vs. baseline
Main branch mode runs the full bench suite (scoped by nx affected), merges fresh results into existing latest.json, and commits via bot commit: 'chore(benchmarks): update benchmark results [skip ci]'
Main branch mode triggers the docs deploy workflow only if results actually changed
Workflow installs Rust toolchain via actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain in both modes
Workflow runs on ubuntu-latest with consistent runner specs
bench-baseline target updates baseline.json and commits it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Docs site Benchmarks page with Chart.js charts
userStories.description: As a user visiting the TypoKit docs, I want to see benchmark results with interactive charts on a dedicated Benchmarks page.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new 'Performance' section to the Starlight sidebar in packages/docs/astro.config.mjs
Create packages/docs/src/content/docs/performance/benchmarks.md as the Benchmarks overview page
Create a custom Astro component (e.g., packages/docs/src/components/BenchmarkCharts.astro) that imports latest.json and renders charts using Chart.js

Charts include: bar chart for JSON scenario req/s (logarithmic scale), bar chart for validation scenario req/s (log scale), bar chart for DB scenario req/s (log scale), bar chart for startup cold-start times, latency percentile comparison table
Include a TypoKit platform×server heatmap showing all 13 combinations (12 JS + Rust/Axum) with Rust/Axum visually distinguished
Charts use logarithmic scale for req/s with captions noting the scale
Charts use a consistent color scheme aligned with TypoKit docs branding
Pages are responsive and readable on mobile
Charts are generated at docs build time from latest.json (static, not client-side fetching)
Add chart.js as a dependency in packages/docs/package.json
Verify in browser if browser testing tools are available

userStories.title: Docs site Methodology page
userStories.description: As a user viewing benchmarks, I want a Methodology page explaining how results were generated so I can reproduce them or understand the context.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/docs/src/content/docs/performance/methodology.md

Document: hardware specs used for published results (CI runner specs), bombardier settings (connections, duration, warmup), what each scenario measures (json, validate, db, middleware, startup, serialization)
Document: how to reproduce locally (prerequisites, pnpm bench command, expected runtime)
Document: how nx affected scoping works (partial runs, cumulative latest.json merge, when benchmarks skip)
Document: how CI regression detection works (baseline.json, 10% threshold, PR comments)
Document: framework list with links and version numbers
Add 'Methodology' entry to the Performance sidebar section in astro.config.mjs
Typecheck passes
